reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all parts of the informal claim: V is a real vector space (AddCommGroup + Module ℝ V), T is a linear operator on V (End ℝ V), "T has no eigenvalues" is encoded as `∀ c : ℝ, eigenspace T c = ⊥` (equivalent to ¬HasEigenvalue since Mathlib has `HasEigenvalue μ ↔ eigenspace μ ≠ ⊥`), T-invariant subspace is expressed as `Submodule.map T U ≤ U` (i.e., T(U) ⊆ U), and "even dimension" is `Even (finrank ℝ U)`. The only addition is `[FiniteDimensional ℝ V]`, which is a natural implicit premise: the informal claim about "even dimension" is only mathematically substantive when the ambient space is finite-dimensional, and this is the standard reading of such dimension-based claims. Making this implicit premise explicit is faithful per the rubric.
